content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the diff against `origin/master`, read the changed `process_backend.rs` and `engine.rs` sections end-to-end, and ran `cargo test --lib process_backend -- --nocapture` (100 tests passed). I found 2 remaining robustness gaps in the new failure-diagnostics paths.

## Amendments

1. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1511-1516` and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1552-1556` still splice `stdout_error` into the `InvocationFailed` detail without any truncation, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5065-5072` now copies that detail verbatim into `runtime/logs/run.ndjson`. `extract_stdout_error()` at `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1695-1724` can join arbitrary-length `result` / `errors[]` strings, so a single backend error can still bloat the runtime log or leak a large prompt echo even though stderr is capped. Truncate `stdout_error` to the same bound as `stderr_tail`, or replace it with a pointer to the preserved `runtime/failed/*.failed.raw` artifact.
   Rationale: The new stderr cap does not actually bound the full runtime-log payload; stdout-side error text is still unbounded on non-zero exits.
2. Two decoder failure paths still miss the new diagnostics entirely: `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:227-239` (`Claude returned empty result with no structured_output`) omits any stderr detail, and `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:334-340` (`failed to read codex last-message file`) omits `stdout_len` / `stderr_len`. Because `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5065-5072` only forwards `error.to_string()`, these branches still produce thin `stage_failed` runtime logs. They should attach the same bounded stderr/raw-length context, or at least the preserved failed-artifact path, that the neighboring invalid-JSON branches now include.
   Rationale: The change set fixes the headline invalid-JSON cases, but operators still hit under-specified runtime logs on sibling failure modes in the same decoder logic.
